nodes.name: Watch FEVER Emails
nodes.notes: Configure IMAP credentials for the inbox that receives FEVER notification emails. Sender: partners@feverup.com. Subject patterns: 'New purchase with Fever for your event {EVENT_NAME}' (bookings) and cancellation/refund notifications.

nodes.name: Route by Type
nodes.notes: Classifies the FEVER email as 'new_booking', 'cancellation', or 'unknown' based on subject line keywords. Unknown emails are dropped (not processed).

nodes.name: New Booking?
nodes.notes: TRUE output → new booking path. FALSE output → check if cancellation.

nodes.name: Is Cancellation?
nodes.notes: TRUE output → cancellation path. FALSE output → unknown email type, skip processing.

nodes.name: Parse FEVER Email Body
nodes.notes: Parses the FEVER purchase notification email. Extracts event name from subject, then parses '{Name} x {qty} {DD/M/YYYY} {time} {price} AUD' from the body text. FEVER does NOT provide customer email or booking reference — both are synthetically generated for dedup and CRM purposes.

nodes.name: Match Event & Session
nodes.notes: Calls the match_event_session RPC to find the matching event_id and session_id. Uses the same RPC function as the SFF and Promotix workflows.

nodes.name: Upsert Customer to CRM
nodes.notes: Upserts the customer into the CRM table using a synthetic placeholder email (fever.{first}.{last}@placeholder.local) since FEVER does not provide real customer emails. Source set to 'fever'.

nodes.name: Prepare Ticket Entry
nodes.notes: Builds the insert payload. commission_rate is 20% for FEVER. gross_revenue comes from the parsed email price. status is set to 'confirmed' for new bookings. Does NOT include commission_amount or net_revenue as those are generated columns.

nodes.name: Insert Ticket Entry
nodes.notes: Inserts the ticket entry with status='confirmed'. The unique index on booking_reference prevents duplicates (409 Conflict). The synthetic FEVER-{namehash}-{date} reference ensures one entry per customer per event date.

nodes.name: Parse Cancellation Email
nodes.notes: Parses the FEVER cancellation/refund email to extract customer name and event date. Reconstructs the synthetic booking reference (FEVER-{firstlast}-{YYYY-MM-DD}) used during the original import, so we can look up and cancel the matching entry.

nodes.name: Cancel Ticket Entry
nodes.notes: PATCHes the matching manual_ticket_entries row to set status='cancelled'. Matches by the reconstructed synthetic booking reference (e.g. FEVER-miatran-2026-06-26). If no matching row exists, the PATCH returns an empty array (no error).

nodes.name: Errors - Review Needed
nodes.notes: Add a Slack or email notification here to alert when FEVER imports have errors.

nodes.name: Skip Unknown Email
nodes.notes: Unknown email type from feverup - neither a new purchase nor a cancellation/refund. No action taken.
